name: Melat Tekie
title: Full-Stack Developer, Frontend Engineer
bio: Developer, designer, data person; in that order depending on the day. I like building things that actually get used.

experience.role: B.S. Computer Science, Minor in Psychology
experience.narrative: I studied Computer Science with a minor in Psychology at Minnesota State University Moorhead, graduating Magna Cum Laude with a 3.69 GPA and making the Dean's List seven out of eight semesters. Outside the classroom, I mentored fourth graders in Scratch programming as a 'code sensei' and supervised a team of thirteen student workers on campus construction projects — early lessons in both teaching and leading.

My first role out of school was building solutions on the Salesforce CRM platform using Apex and JavaScript. I learned the real rhythms of professional development here — daily standups, sprint planning, and managing support tickets through Jira.

I stretched into new territory here — developing iOS apps in Objective-C and Xcode, integrating an e-commerce site with Klaviyo through PHP, and building on Dynamics 365 CRM with C#, Java, and jQuery. I also connected ConnectWise with other ticketing systems via its SDK, my first real taste of systems integration.

Contracting for Microsoft, I analyzed quarterly reports and built Macros to automate recurring processes. Working with SQL, SAS, Python, and Power BI on large datasets taught me how to turn raw numbers into decisions people could actually act on.

experience.role: M.S. Business Analytics & Graduate Research Associate II
experience.narrative: While earning my Master's, I worked as a Graduate Research Associate — maintaining Sitecore CMS components, building Power BI and Python reporting dashboards, and optimizing site performance and SEO. I was honored with the Wayne Watts Fellowship for this work.

Today, I sit between stakeholders and engineering — building custom SharePoint web parts, running QA on live applications, designing UX prototypes in Figma, and writing governance standards for Power Platform development. It's the role where design, data, and development finally come together.

projects.name: Provider Audit Tool
projects.description: Auditing whether medical staff met initial credentialing and reappointment criteria was a manual, math-heavy process prone to error. I built a full Power Apps application that automates the eligibility calculations, with role-based permissions controlling what each user sees and can act on. When an audit item fails or needs review, an Outlook integration automatically sends the responsible user a list of flagged items with the ability to act directly — and if the back-and-forth drags on, the system automatically escalates to a manager. What used to be manual, error-prone tracking is now a self-enforcing workflow.

projects.name: University Sitecore Migration
projects.description: The university department's site lived in Dreamweaver, disconnected from the university's actual CMS. I led the migration into Sitecore, rebuilding the site's structure and content within it, then implemented Siteimprove to continuously monitor and enforce state accessibility compliance — catching issues before they became legal or usability problems instead of after.

projects.name: Salesforce Forms & Analytics for Pharma Client
projects.description: A pharmaceutical client needed structured data collection built directly into their Salesforce environment. I built the forms themselves, then integrated Einstein Analytics using Python to turn that data into real reporting the team could act on, instead of static entries sitting unused in the CRM.

projects.name: Klaviyo Integration for Heatwave
projects.description: Heatwave, a custom apparel company built around logo tee campaigns, had no way to see whether their marketing was actually working. I integrated Klaviyo into their Laravel/PHP site to track engagement and churn, giving them real visibility into impressions and conversions per campaign — turning marketing spend from a guess into a measured decision.

projects.name: ServiceNow ↔ ConnectWise Ticket Integration
projects.description: After an acquisition, the company was running two separate help desk systems — ServiceNow and the acquired entity's ConnectWise — with no way to see tickets from both in one place. I built an integration so a ticket submitted on either platform appears seamlessly alongside all the others, regardless of which system a customer happened to use. To the end user, it looks and feels like one unified service desk.

projects.name: ServiceNow Portal UX Redesign
projects.description: The client's ServiceNow portal was limited to out-of-the-box components only — no custom code allowed. Working entirely within Page Designer, I experimented with layout and component combinations to design the best possible user experience the platform's constraints would allow, proving that a limitation doesn't have to mean a bad experience.

projects.name: SharePoint Communication Sites
projects.description: Built a multilingual SharePoint site serving both English and Spanish-speaking users, ensuring content and navigation worked seamlessly in both languages. I also built a suite of custom web parts used across the organization's communication sites, giving other teams reusable, on-brand components instead of rebuilding the same functionality repeatedly.

projects.name: Catering Platform
projects.description: A local restaurant needed a way to take catering orders online instead of relying on phone calls and guesswork. I built a full ordering system in Next.js with a live Supabase backend — customers browse the real menu, use a headcount calculator to see pricing update in real time, and submit inquiries that land directly in the owner's inbox. The result: a self-serve ordering flow that didn't exist before, cutting out manual back-and-forth for every quote.

projects.name: Portfolio Engine
projects.description: Building a portfolio for every future client from scratch would mean rewriting the same page over and over. Instead, I designed a single Astro-based engine driven entirely by JSON — each person's name, bio, skills, and projects live in their own data file, and one dynamic route renders any of them on demand. Adding a new client's portfolio now means duplicating a template and filling in their info, not building a new site from zero.
